sn12.65:1.2: "Before my awakening, *bhikkhus*, when I was still an unawakened bodhisatta, this occurred to me:
sn12.65:1.3: ‘Alas, this world has fallen into difficulty: it is born, ages, dies, passes away, and arises again.
sn12.65:1.4: And yet it does not understand the escape from this *dukkha*, from aging-and-death.
sn12.65:1.5: When indeed will an escape from this *dukkha*, from aging-and-death, be discerned?’
sn12.65:1.7: ‘When what exists does aging-and-death come to be? With what as condition is aging-and-death?’
sn12.65:1.8: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikārā*], there was for me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññāya*]:
sn12.65:1.9: ‘When birth exists, aging-and-death comes to be; with birth as condition, aging-and-death.’
sn12.65:2.11: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikārā*], there was for me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññāya*]:
sn12.65:2.12: ‘When *viññāṇa* exists, *nāmarūpa* comes to be; with *viññāṇa* as condition, *nāmarūpa*.’
sn12.65:2.14: ‘When what exists does *viññāṇa* come to be? With what as condition is *viññāṇa*?’
sn12.65:2.15: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikārā*], there was for me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññāya*]:
sn12.65:2.16: ‘When *nāmarūpa* exists, *viññāṇa* comes to be; with *nāmarūpa* as condition, *viññāṇa*.’
sn12.65:2.2: ‘When what exists does birth come to be …
sn12.65:2.8: does dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatanaṁ*] come to be …
sn12.65:3.2: this *viññāṇa* turns back from *nāmarūpa*; it does not go beyond.
sn12.65:3.3: To this extent one might be born, age, die, pass away, or arise again, namely: with *nāmarūpa* as condition, *viññāṇa*;
sn12.65:3.5: with *nāmarūpa* as condition, dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatanaṁ*];
sn12.65:3.6: with dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as condition, contact …
sn12.65:3.7: Such is the arising of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
sn12.65:3.8: ‘Arising, arising’: thus, *bhikkhus*, in regard to *dhammas* never heard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn12.65:4.13: does contact not come to be …
sn12.65:4.14: does dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatanaṁ*] not come to be …
sn12.65:4.16: With the cessation of what is there cessation of *nāmarūpa*?’
sn12.65:4.17: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikārā*], there was for me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññāya*]:
sn12.65:4.18: ‘When *viññāṇa* does not exist, *nāmarūpa* does not come to be;
sn12.65:4.19: with the cessation of *viññāṇa*, cessation of *nāmarūpa*.’
sn12.65:4.2: ‘When what does not exist does aging-and-death not come to be;
sn12.65:4.3: with the cessation of what is there cessation of aging-and-death?’
sn12.65:4.4: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikārā*], there was for me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññāya*]:
sn12.65:4.5: ‘When birth does not exist, aging-and-death does not come to be;
sn12.65:4.6: with the cessation of birth, cessation of aging-and-death.’
sn12.65:4.8: ‘When what does not exist does birth not come to be …
sn12.65:5.2: ‘When what does not exist does *viññāṇa* not come to be;
sn12.65:5.3: with the cessation of what is there cessation of *viññāṇa*?’
sn12.65:5.4: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikārā*], there was for me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññāya*]:
sn12.65:5.5: ‘When *nāmarūpa* does not exist, *viññāṇa* does not come to be;
sn12.65:5.6: with the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, cessation of *viññāṇa*.’
sn12.65:6.2: this path to awakening has been attained by me, namely—
sn12.65:6.3: with the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, cessation of *viññāṇa*;
sn12.65:6.4: with the cessation of *viññāṇa*, cessation of *nāmarūpa*;
sn12.65:6.5: with the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, cessation of dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
sn12.65:6.6: with the cessation of dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], cessation of contact …
sn12.65:6.7: Such is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
sn12.65:6.8: ‘Cessation, cessation’: thus, *bhikkhus*, in regard to *dhammas* never heard before, vision arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowledge arose, light arose.
sn12.65:7.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, a man wandering in a forest wilderness were to see an ancient road, an ancient path, traveled by people of the past.
sn12.65:7.10: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, I saw an ancient road, an ancient path, traveled by rightly self-awakened ones of the past.
sn12.65:7.3: Following it, he would see an ancient city, an ancient royal capital, inhabited by people of the past, furnished with parks, furnished with groves, furnished with lotus ponds, with ramparts, delightful.
sn12.65:7.4: Then, *bhikkhus*, that man would report it to a king or a king’s chief minister:
sn12.65:7.5: ‘Please know this, sir: while wandering in a forest wilderness, I saw an ancient road, an ancient path, traveled by people of the past, and I followed it.
sn12.65:7.6: Following it, I saw an ancient city, an ancient royal capital, inhabited by people of the past, furnished with parks, furnished with groves, furnished with lotus ponds, with ramparts, delightful.
sn12.65:7.8: Then, *bhikkhus*, that king or king’s chief minister would build that city.
sn12.65:7.9: After some time, that city would become prosperous and flourishing, populous, crowded with people, grown and expanded.
sn12.65:8.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is that ancient road, that ancient path, traveled by rightly self-awakened ones of the past?
sn12.65:8.10: following it, I directly knew birth …
sn12.65:8.16: I directly knew dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatanaṁ*] …
sn12.65:8.2: It is this noble eightfold path, that is—
sn12.65:8.21: I directly knew the arising of *saṅkhāras*;
sn12.65:8.22: I directly knew the cessation of *saṅkhāras*;
sn12.65:8.23: I directly knew the practice leading to the cessation of *saṅkhāras*.
sn12.65:8.24: Having directly known that, I taught it to *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhunis*, laymen, and laywomen.
sn12.65:8.25: And now, *bhikkhus*, this holy life has become prosperous and flourishing, widespread, popular, extensive, well proclaimed as far as *devas* and humans."
sn12.65:8.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is that ancient road, that ancient path, traveled by rightly self-awakened ones of the past; I followed it.
sn12.65:8.5: Following it, I directly knew aging-and-death;
sn12.65:8.6: I directly knew the arising of aging-and-death;
sn12.65:8.7: I directly knew the cessation of aging-and-death;
sn12.65:8.8: I directly knew the practice leading to the cessation of aging-and-death.
